findings.evidence: `Cylinder.lean:41` has `def prefixCylinder ... {ω | ∀ i : Fin r, X i ω ∈ C i}` and `Cylinder.lean:60` has `def indProd ... X i ω`; nearby general API is `Basic.lean:37 def blockLaw ... (k : Fin m → ℕ)`, and the roadmap states the bridge is for every finite injective selection `k : Fin m → ℕ` (`TauCetiRoadmap/Exchangeability/README.md:333-335`).
findings.fix: Define the general versions first, e.g. `blockCylinder X k C := {ω | ∀ i, X (k i) ω ∈ C i}` and `blockIndProd X k C`, prove the measurability/integrability/indicator lemmas there, and derive the prefix API by taking `k := fun i => i.val`.
findings.issue: `prefixCylinder`/`indProd` only cover `X i` for `i : Fin r`; the de Finetti factorization target is over finite selections `k : Fin m → ℕ`, matching existing `blockLaw`, so this bakes in a prefix special case.

summary: The cylinder/indicator API is under-generalized to prefixes, while the surrounding exchangeability API and roadmap use arbitrary finite coordinate selections.
